senses.gloss: to make a cut or gap in; to cut into or through; to tear through; to transpierce; to cut into or through (something hard or rigid, for example, a skull, a roof, and so on, or a mass, for example, a rock), to bore through; to crack, to fissure

to make a cut or gap in; to cut into or through; to tear through; to transpierce; to cut into or through (something soft or pliable, for example, skin or a skin-covered body part such as a finger, a vein, flesh, or cloth or fabric, or a thickness or layer, for example, a paper, a sheet, or a leaf), to incise, to slit, to gash, to slash, to rip
to cut through (something seemingly flat or level, such as a surface) parallelly;
to cut through (something seemingly flat or level, such as a surface) parallelly;; to make a recess in; to groove; to furrow; to indent
to cut in two or asunder, to tear apart, to split, to cleave
to cut in two or asunder, to tear apart, to split, to cleave; to spread or cause disagreement or factionalism in or among; to divide, to rive
to move or pass through; to cut across; to traverse, to cross

examples.text: لِلنَّجَاحِ، يَجِبُ عَلَى الْمَرْءِ أَنْ يَشُقَّ طَرِيقًا حَتَّى عِنْدَمَا تَظْهَرُ التَّحَدِّيَاتُ.
examples.english: To succeed, one must forge a path even when challenges arise.

senses.gloss: to break or break forth, to come up, to erupt, to emerge
to be or become burdensome [with عَلَى (ʕalā) ‘for’], to be or become hard to bear for; to weigh down
a gap; a gash; a fissure; a hollow, a cranny
a recess; an indenture; a furrow

examples.text: وأوضح أنه “ليس خافياً أنه كلف المدير العام للأمن العام اللواء عباس إبراهيم إدارة هذا الشق من الحوار الرسمي بين البلدين، بإزاء ما يرتبط بهذين الملفين”.
examples.english: He elucidated that “it isn’t a secret that he instructed the Director-General of Public Security, Major General Abbas Ibrahim, to manage this part of the official dialogue between the two countries, with respect to what is connected with these two files.”

etymology.text: See the etymology of the corresponding lemma form.

body.text: periences difficulty in striving to master him.

in a state of straitness, distress, adversity,
And I have not been, in supplicating Thee, my Lord, unprosperous
